creator: amdmi3@amdmi3.ru
text:
* gerald@FreeBSD.org (gerald@FreeBSD.org) wrote:

> I have enhanced emulators/wine with the following options over the 
> course of the last months:
>  
>   OPTIONS=CUPS    "Use CUPS (Common UNIX Printing System)"        On \
>           HAL     "Use HAL (Hardware Abstraction Layer)"          Off \
>           LDAP    "Use LDAP"                                      Off \
>           LIBXSLT "Use libxslt (only used by msxml3.dll)"         Off
> 
> Wine internally has several additional knobs, but those four above seem
> to be the most important ones and combinatorial explosion is not exactly
> desirable.
> 
> If you have a good reason for some other knob, or to add some explicit
> --without-FOO, please let me know and I'll look into it.

I think that in any case knob or explicit disabling of something that
changes list of depends is better than introducing a hidden dependency.

Combinatorial explosion is not exactly a problem, as you usually don't
need to test each and any combination of options, but at least knowing
the options set is better than deducing the cause of problem with hidden
depends.

Anyway thanks for adding those opts. 

--
